Title: My Take On God and Religion
Body:
[justify] Being the science enthusiast that I am, I have heard a lot of discussions, debates and understandings about science, space and the Universe. Along with that, I have kept a similar attitude and keen eye towards subjects like spirituality, religion and the classic, God. The two diverse sets of topics that I have mentioned, are looked at as contraries by most, and associative by some. Many put their complete faith on God and religion while keeping the scientific framework as secondary or even inferior at times. Since I live in a culturally Hindu household in a country like India the entirety of my life, I have been brought up around religious practices, sentiments, and mindsets. I have seen with the closest of my capacities, how people around me keep the strongest of faiths in their religion, in their tradition and obviously, in their God. Personally, I did not seem to have any sort of issue with the same whatsoever even with the scientific temperament of mine. Though, I will not deny that at times, one’s faith can cause pain to a scientific mind, and might as well seem illogical. I suppose, that is the reason we call it “faith” instead of “fact”. Therefore, resilience and acceptance are the reasons why I nominate myself as a candidate to simplify the intersection between Science and God. [/justify]

[justify} I will try to be as direct as I possibly can without hurting anybody’s feelings, unless someone reject’s science as a whole; they can close this article this instant. It does not matter whether one prioritizes faith over science or not, one thing that remains constant is that science leads one’s way in life. Faith may shape your thinking and your actions, but it will always be science that will get you going in our everyday tasks. [/justify]
